Hello Wako,

We're glad the order total for #12125 is now showing correctly.

To explain what's happening: when an order is edited in Shopify after it has already been imported into the app (for example, adjusting the shipping cost), those changes are not automatically reflected in the Multi-Carrier app. The app retains the original order data from the time of import. This is the reason you're seeing a mismatch whenever you modify the shipping cost post-order.

For future occurrences, the steps Sagar followed are the correct workaround:
1. Select the affected order in the app.
2. Click the three dots and change the status to Initial.
3. Click Import Orders to pull in the updated order data from Shopify.

Thank you for flagging that this happens consistently when shipping costs are edited after order placement — we appreciate the specific feedback. We'll note this as a product improvement request so the team is aware of the need for better handling of post-order edits.

If you run into any further issues, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

Hello,

This is fantastic, thank you.

I have one question regarding the **Packing Summary** feature.

We often ship multiple parcels for a single order, and my employees manually assign which items are packed into each box by editing "packing summary".

However, when the order is updated (often due to shipping cost adjustments), the Packing Summary refreshes, and all parcel assignments are lost. We then have to recreate the packing information from scratch, which is very time-consuming.

Is there a way to preserve the Packing Summary and parcel assignments when an order is updated? This would save us a significant amount of manual work and help prevent errors.

Thank you for your help.

Kind regards,
Wako Chapurlat
MAJO GARN

email wako@majogarn.com

Web www.majogarn.com

Hello Wako,

Thank you for raising this — it is a very important workflow question.

Unfortunately, preserving the Packing Summary across order updates is not currently supported. When an order is updated in Shopify and the app refreshes its data, the packaging is recalculated from scratch, and any manual parcel assignments you have made are reset. There is no setting at this time to lock or retain custom box assignments when an order syncs.

One thing worth being aware of: with the Auto Order Update feature being activated for your store, order changes from Shopify will sync to the app automatically. This will solve the order total discrepancy issue going forward, but it does mean the Packing Summary will refresh automatically whenever an order is edited in Shopify. Depending on how frequently your team edits shipping costs, this could make the repacking step more frequent.

We understand this adds manual effort and risk of error for multi-parcel shipments. If you would like, we can log the ability to preserve manual packing assignments across order updates as a feature request with our product team. Please let us know and we will pass this along.

In the meantime, if you have any other questions, please feel free to reach out.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

Hello Wako,

Thank you for raising this. We understand the shipment to South Korea is blocked, and we want to get this resolved for you as quickly as possible.

The Auto Order Update feature that was enabled for your store helps sync future order edits from Shopify into the app automatically. However, it does not retroactively update a commercial invoice or label that has already been generated — which is why order #12125 still shows the incorrect shipping value of 878.87 EUR instead of 394 EUR.

To resolve this specific order, here is what we recommend:

1. Void the existing label for order #12125 in the app (More Actions > Void Label). This will also discard the current commercial invoice.
2. Confirm that the order in Shopify shows the correct shipping cost of 394 EUR before regenerating.
3. Once the order values in Shopify are confirmed correct, regenerate the label and commercial invoice from the app.

Regarding why the shipping cost is appearing doubled in the commercial invoice — this needs to be reviewed by our team. We would like to investigate whether a setting in your carrier or commercial invoice configuration is causing the shipping line to be counted twice. Could you please share the following so we can look into this right away:

- A screenshot of the app's order view for #12125 showing the shipping and total values currently displayed
- The carrier being used for this South Korea shipment (e.g., DHL Express, FedEx, etc.)

We will prioritise this given the shipment is time-sensitive.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive

Hello Wako,

Thank you for flagging this. The discrepancy you are seeing is because the commercial invoice you have was generated before the order total correction was applied to the underlying order data. The PDF captured the incorrect values at the time it was produced.

To get an accurate commercial invoice, please follow these steps:

1. In the MCSL app, locate order #12125.
2. Delete or void the existing commercial invoice that was generated with the incorrect values.
3. Regenerate the commercial invoice. This will pull the current, corrected order data (€3,128.00 total, with the correct shipping cost of €394.00).

The regenerated invoice should reflect the correct totals and you will be able to proceed with the shipment. If after regenerating the invoice still shows €3,612.87 or any doubled shipping cost, please let us know immediately and we will escalate this for further investigation on our end.

Thanks & Regards,

The Bottom Line: Despite following previous instructions to refresh orders, the app appears to aggregate all previous shipping entries in the JSON data rather than replacing them. For example:

  - Test Order #12226: This test order initially had €52.29 in shipping costs. After I deleted that entry and added €100, the commercial invoice incorrectly totaled €152.29 and invoice total is now €752.29, instead of correct €700.00. 
  - Order #12161: The invoice incorrectly totaled €1,517.19 for shipping cost because it included multiple deleted entries.
   - Order #12125: The same issue happened as above.

I look forward to your confirmation on whether a permanent fix has been implemented.

    Kind regards, Wako Chapurlat  MAJO GARN

Hello Wako,

Thank you for creating test order #12226 and documenting the steps so clearly — this is exactly the kind of evidence our team needs.

To directly answer your question: a permanent fix has not yet been deployed. Test order #12226 confirms the issue is still present. The pattern you described — €52.29 (deleted) + €100 (new) = €152.29 on the commercial invoice — is consistent with what our team identified when investigating orders #12161 and #12125.

What our team found is that when a shipping cost is deleted and a new one is added through Shopify's order editing flow, Shopify retains all historical shipping entries in the order data. The app currently reads and sums all of these accumulated entries rather than only the current active shipping cost, which causes the commercial invoice total to be overstated.

This issue was escalated to our development team on June 16 for investigation and a permanent fix. We do not yet have a confirmed resolution date, but we will update you in this ticket as soon as the fix is deployed.

In the meantime, we understand you have been creating labels directly on the FedEx website for affected orders — we appreciate your patience in working around this while the fix is in progress.

Thanks & Regards,
Team PluginHive
